title: Anduril's Barracuda Missile and the Race for Lethal Autonomous Weapons
content:
Anduril is hoping its new Barracuda missile will gain significant traction as a long-range strike weapon. A new world order is emerging, defined by algorithms, shifting alliances, and a rupture in geopolitical fault lines. Amid rerouted supply chains and the battle for computing power, business leaders must turn complexity into a competitive advantage. Executives and policymakers shaping what comes next are not waiting for certainty in this volatile era. Instead, they are building new defense strategies for a world defined by its absence.

The push for autonomous weapons represents a major shift in modern military strategy. As technology rapidly advances, the integration of artificial intelligence into lethal systems has sparked intense debate. Proponents argue that autonomous weapons provide crucial speed and precision in complex combat scenarios. However, critics warn of the profound ethical implications of removing human oversight from lethal decisions. The outcome of this race will likely reshape the future of warfare.
